title: Greeks' Secret Pillowy Gratin
description: A flower-scented, hangover-fighting gratin that pairs unexpectedly well with crispy fried eggs or creamy tzatziki. Rich, pillowy Greek flour puffed to perfection within ttukbaegi's earthenware heart.

ingredients.name: 1 small onion, finely chopped
ingredients.notes: any color, but white for a more neutral flavor

instructions: Prepare ttukbaegi by applying a thin layer of olive oil to the interior.
In a mixing bowl, combine self-raising flour, grated kefalograviera cheese, parmesan cheese, chopped fresh dill, dried oregano, salt, and black pepper. Mix until well combined.
Add chopped onion and mix until evenly distributed throughout the flour mixture.
Pour in the desired amount of olive oil and mix until the dough forms a uniform ball.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes to allow the flour to hydrate.

Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces and shape each piece into a ball. Flatten each ball into a disk using your palms. Place the disks into the prepared ttukbaegi, leaving about 1 inch of space between each disk.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes or until the edges are golden brown and the centers are set.
Remove from oven and let cool for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve warm, garnished with additional fresh dill if desired.
Store leftovers in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 3 days or freeze for up to 2 months.
